Now that we have basic Discord knowledge from the previous post, of course we have to build something. Won't be too hard — mostly stuff we've written and used before.
To make this easier to practice, I'll provide the relevant code and commands. Set up the project:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

mkdir example-discord-bot-crawler
cd example-discord-bot-crawler
npm init -y
touch index.js
touch register.js
touch .env
Then install the packages we'll need:
I won't repeat the basic code from before — just paste it:
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
language-env shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

# .env
DISCORD_TOKEN=Your Discord Token
DISCORD_CLIENT_ID=Your Discord Client ID

When you call commands, always use
to reply to the user. Using

Now to implement the auto-send-an-article-every-9-PM feature. We'll use the
package — lets us run code on a schedule.
to run every minute first, for testing. If everything's good, change it to:
// You can change Channel ID to process.env.DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID
Getting the Channel ID is easy — right-click the channel in Discord and click "Copy ID".
Now after starting the bot, every night at 9 PM it auto-sends an article title and link.
